stories.body: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard says it has hit U.S. facilities across Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman and Jordan while enforcing a shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz, prompting American strikes on Iranian air defenses, radar and naval assets and pushing oil prices above their early-war highs; traffic through the strait has fallen by half, shippers report, even as Washington escorts convoys and insists the waterway remains open, and both Donald Trump and Tehran’s negotiators now declare last month’s truce over.
stories.headline: US and Iran trade new strikes as Tehran moves to close the Strait of Hormuz

stories.body: European allies meeting in Paris pledged to co-produce drones with Kyiv and to deploy a multinational stabilization mission after any cease-fire, prompting Russia’s Foreign Ministry to warn that any foreign contingent on Ukrainian soil would be treated as a legitimate target, underscoring Moscow’s opposition to outside security guarantees even as Ukraine seeks new defense industry partnerships to replenish its forces.
stories.headline: EU backs joint drone production with Ukraine as Russia warns foreign troops would be targets

stories.body: China’s economy grew 4.3% year-on-year between April and June, undershooting Beijing’s 4.5–5% goal despite a 27% surge in June exports driven by semiconductors and electric vehicles; officials cite persistent domestic weakness, falling property prices and shrinking fixed-asset investment, while analysts say lowering the target has simply exposed pre-existing fragility even as retailers and manufacturers absorb higher energy costs caused by the Iran war’s impact on oil prices.
stories.headline: China’s second-quarter growth misses target as weak demand offsets export boom

stories.body: London plans to make major platforms such as Instagram, TikTok and YouTube inaccessible by default to 16- and 17-year-olds between midnight and 6 a.m. while disabling autoplay and infinite scroll, but teens could opt out and ministers still must define “social media” under the Online Safety Act, leaving lawyers warning that marketplaces or other user-generated services might be swept in and incoming prime minister Andy Burnham facing unresolved questions about age checks and carve-outs.
stories.headline: UK weighs midnight social media curfew for 16- and 17-year-olds amid enforcement questions

stories.body: Immigration and Customs Enforcement temporarily halted most vehicle stops following a second fatal shooting in a week, this one of Colombian national Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero during an operation in Biddeford, Maine, sparking protests and vigils even as President Donald Trump publicly urges agents to restart aggressive tactics that have already produced at least nine deaths during his expanded immigration crackdown.
stories.headline: ICE traffic stops suspended after two deadly shootings but Trump pressures agents to resume

stories.body: House Speaker Mike Johnson outlined a 47-page budget framework that would add $95 billion for Pentagon operations in the Iran war theater, bolster farm programs and tighten voter registration requirements without offsetting the new spending, reviving elements of last year’s Trump-backed tax-and-cut agenda while courting conservatives wary of the deficit impact.
stories.headline: House GOP unveils $95 billion plan tying Iran war funds to farm aid and election rules

stories.body: Senators from both parties are expected to use Jay Clayton’s confirmation hearing for director of national intelligence to air proposals to curb or even dismantle the Office of the DNI after turbulent tenures by Tulsi Gabbard and interim chief Bill Pulte, with Democrats demanding assurances he will resist politicization and explain Justice Department subpoenas to reporters while Republicans debate whether trimming the spy bureaucracy would improve intelligence operations.
stories.headline: Jay Clayton heads into contentious DNI hearing amid calls to shrink the agency

stories.body: A provincial court in Badajoz sentenced Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s brother David to nine years of disqualification for administrative malfeasance tied to his 2017 hiring by the provincial council, sparking jubilation in the opposition Partido Popular, which frames the ruling as proof of systemic Socialist corruption even as judges dismissed the more serious influence-trafficking charges at the heart of the Manos Limpias complaint.
stories.headline: Court bans prime minister’s brother David Sánchez from office over patronage job

stories.body: Brussels, London, Madrid and Gibraltar signed a 336-article post-Brexit treaty that removes the century-old border fence, installs dual controls at the airport and port, raises Gibraltar’s VAT-equivalent tax to at least 15% and launches shared customs management while preserving Spain’s sovereignty claims, prompting Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez to hail “the last wall in continental Europe” coming down even as PP and Vox denounce the pact as a concession to British military interests.
stories.headline: EU-UK deal dismantles Gibraltar fence and creates joint border controls

stories.body: At the European People’s Party Libertas Forum in Madrid, PP leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo described Pedro Sánchez as a president who mistrusts judges, disregards parliamentary majorities and weakens institutions, warning delegates that Spain’s democracy is “debilitated” even as Vox amplified corruption accusations and the government countered with claims of opposition lawfare following the sentencing of the prime minister’s brother.
stories.headline: Feijóo brands Sánchez “authoritarian” before European conservatives

stories.body: The July CIS barometer, based on 4,020 interviews conducted July 1–6, estimates the PSOE at 33% of the vote, up 1.7 points, while the PP falls to 25.1%, narrowing Vox to 15.3% and Sumar to 6.1%; ERC jumps to 2.7% and Junts slips to 0.7%, suggesting the Socialists have weathered the convictions of José Luis Ábalos and other corruption probes even as housing and immigration top public concerns.
stories.headline: CIS poll gives PSOE eight-point lead over PP despite scandals

stories.body: Territory Minister Sílvia Paneque said the Generalitat will keep funding temporary housing for families evacuated after a construction void on Barcelona’s L9 metro line caused a street-level collapse, is considering compensation for residents and shops, and is alternating tunneling work with daily inspections and soil injections while investigators probe whether insufficient concrete filled the cavity during earlier drilling.
stories.headline: Catalan government readies compensation options for residents displaced by L9 sinkhole

stories.body: Barcelona’s citywide neighborhood federation FAVB says the municipal plan to end holiday-rental licenses by 2028 contains loopholes that could let owners delay compliance by filing lawsuits, converting entire buildings to hotels or student residences, or requesting five-year extensions for units that received major investment, and is urging stepped-up inspections, sanctions, license withdrawals and anti-speculation measures to guarantee more than 10,000 tourist apartments return to residential use.
stories.headline: Activists warn loopholes could undermine 2028 phase-out of tourist flats

stories.body: The city’s Presidency and Security Commission advanced a regulation authorizing Guardia Urbana officers to carry Tasers starting next summer, with PSC, Junts and the PP backing rules that require exhaustive training, limit use to life-threatening situations, ban deployment against minors under 14, pregnant women or detainees, and mandate clear warnings while BComú and ERC oppose the measure and Vox abstained.
stories.headline: Barcelona approves taser rollout for its municipal police

stories.body: Catalan civil protection has activated alerts through at least Wednesday as temperatures again top 40°C inland, Saharan dust blankets the region and Barcelona records highs above 35°C with nighttime lows potentially staying above 27°C, prompting authorities to open cooling centers, warn the elderly and outdoor workers, and restrict access to high-risk natural areas amid extreme wildfire danger in 72 municipalities.
stories.headline: Catalonia endures third heatwave as Barcelona faces stifling nights
